title: Rich Australian Prunes Bowl
description: A hearty, grassy bowl featuring rehydrated prunes, steamed vegetables, and a side of fluffy rice. Serve hot in a dolsot stone bowl for a low-fat-friendly monsoon meal.
instructions: Combine prunes and water in a large bowl. Let soak for at least 4 hours or overnight.
Drain and rinse the prunes. Set aside.
In a medium saucepan, bring the flavorful broth to a simmer over low heat.
Add the short-grain rice and cook, stirring occasionally, until the liquid is absorbed and the rice is fluffy, about 18 minutes.
While the rice cooks, steam the mixed vegetables until tender. Set aside.
In a small saucepan, melt the ghee or low-fat oil over low heat.
Add the grass-fed butter, if using. Stir until melted and fragrant.

To assemble the bowls, divide the cooked rice among four dolsot stone bowls. Top with the rehydrated prunes, steamed vegetables, and a sprinkle of salt and black pepper. Drizzle with the flavorful broth and melted butter, if using.
Garnish with edible flowers, if desired.
Check the seasoning and add more salt or pepper as needed.
